title: Design and implement enterprise-grade support ticket system with AdminV2 integration
issue_description:
Design, investigate, implement, and validate a full enterprise-grade support system for Algonney, similar in quality and flow to Binance and other large platforms. The system must use a proper ticket-based architecture where every support request creates a unique support ticket ID and all messages belong to that ticket. Work step by step with one and only one step per cycle. Start by investigating the current support/chat/help/contact implementation across frontend, backend, database, websocket or polling flow, and AdminV2. Identify what already exists, what is missing, and the exact end-to-end path required for a professional support workflow. Then design and implement the complete solution.

Required product behavior:
When a user opens support chat in Algonney and sends a help request, the system must create a new support ticket with a unique ticket_id/support_id, store the first message, assign the correct user and metadata, and make the ticket appear immediately in the AdminV2 support page. Admin agents must be able to open the ticket, read the conversation, reply, update status, and see all ticket details. User replies and admin replies must flow bi-directionally and remain attached to the same ticket thread. The ticket must persist across refreshes and sessions.

Required architecture:
Use a ticket model and a message model. Ticket should include fields such as ticket_id, user_id, subject or inferred title, category, priority, status, source, created_at, updated_at, last_message_at, assigned_admin_id, unread counts, last_message_preview, and resolution metadata. Message records should include message_id, ticket_id, sender_type, sender_id, body, attachments, created_at, delivery/read metadata, and internal/admin visibility flags if needed. Build the system so it is scalable, auditable, and production-grade.

Required states and behavior:
Implement professional ticket states such as open, pending_admin, pending_user, resolved, and closed. Decide and document exact transition rules. New user message on a new ticket should create/open the ticket and place it in admin inbox immediately. New user reply on an existing resolved ticket should either reopen the same ticket or create a follow-up ticket only if clearly justified by existing architecture; choose the safest professional design and document it. Admin reply should update status appropriately. Include unread indicators for both sides if architecture allows. Preserve a full conversation history.

AdminV2 requirements:
Create or complete the AdminV2 Support page so admins can see a professional inbox/list view and a ticket detail view. The list should support status filters, search, sorting by latest activity, unread indicators, user identity, ticket ID, category, priority, and assignee if applicable. The detail page should show the full conversation timeline, user info, ticket metadata, status controls, and reply composer. Make the UX clean, fast, and enterprise-grade.

User-side requirements:
User support UI should feel like a modern exchange/platform support center. It should show ticket ID clearly, conversation history, ticket status, timestamps, and reply box. If a live chat model is not fully supported, implement robust near-real-time updates using the best existing architecture available, such as websocket, SSE, or safe polling fallback. Do not fake real-time behavior; implement the actual delivery/update mechanism end-to-end.

Investigation and implementation rules:
First inspect current code and prove the current support-related flow and gaps before changing architecture. Reuse existing patterns where correct, but do not force a weak design if it prevents enterprise-grade ticketing. Changes are allowed only after the current state is understood and the missing pieces are clearly identified. Keep the work moving forward; do not repeat previous steps or loop on the same check. Prefer contained, production-safe changes with clear schema, API, backend, and frontend ownership.

Validation requirements:
At the end, prove the system works end-to-end. Validate that:
1. User creates a support request and receives a unique ticket/support ID.
2. Ticket appears in AdminV2 support inbox immediately.
3. Admin opens the ticket and sees the correct user message history.
4. Admin reply is persisted and visible to the user.
5. User reply stays on the same ticket thread.
6. Status transitions behave correctly.
7. Refresh/reload does not lose the conversation.
8. Sorting, filtering, and unread/update behavior work correctly.
9. Database records, API responses, and UI contract all match.
10. No duplicate ticket creation occurs for the same intended flow.

Output expectations:
Work in strict step-by-step mode with one step per cycle. For each step, inspect the single highest-priority part of the system, report findings, then move to the next most important unresolved gap. When the root architecture and missing pieces are proven, implement the full support ticket system end-to-end and validate it thoroughly. Stop only when the support system is fully working, clearly blocked, or requires explicit human approval for a risky production action.
issue_seed_prompt: Design and implement enterprise-grade support ticket system with AdminV2 integration

history.step_title: Inventory existing support-related code paths end-to-end
history.prompt_for_claude:
Investigate the current support-related implementation across the codebase in read-only mode only. Do not change any code.

Goal for this step:
Create a complete inventory of everything that currently exists for support/chat/help/contact/ticket/message/AdminV2 support across frontend, backend, database models/migrations, and realtime update mechanisms.

Scope:

User-facing support/help/contact/chat UI

AdminV2 support page/components/hooks

Backend routes/controllers/services for support/contact/chat/messages/tickets

Database schemas/models/migrations for support/contact/chat/messages/tickets

Any websocket/SSE/polling/subscription mechanism that could be used for near-real-time updates

Any notification/unread counter patterns already used elsewhere that may be relevant

What to do:

Search the repo for keywords such as:
support, ticket, tickets, chat, message, messages, contact, help, inbox, conversation, thread, threads, websocket, ws, sse, polling, unread, admin support

Identify the exact relevant files and summarize each file’s role in 1–2 lines.

Identify current end-to-end paths, if any:

user creates support/help/contact request

backend receives/stores it

admin sees it in AdminV2

replies flow back to user

Explicitly state:

what already exists

what is partially implemented

what is completely missing

whether there is any existing ticket model/message model

whether there is any current realtime or polling flow usable for support

Do not propose a full redesign yet. This step is only about proving the current state.

Output format:

SUMMARY

FILES (full paths)

CURRENT FLOW

EXISTING COMPONENTS

MISSING PIECES

RISKS / NOTES

RECOMMENDED NEXT INVESTIGATION STEP

Keep this step strictly investigative and read-only.
history.claude_output:
SUMMARY:
The codebase has extensive real-time infrastructure (WebSocket, inbox state, unread counters) and a near-complete ticket-style system built for custom strategy requests, but zero dedicated support ticket system: the user-facing support widget is fully mocked/disconnected from
any backend, the backend support route only emails a mailbox, and AdminV2 has no support/inbox section at all.

---
FILES:

Backend:
- /algonney/BRIDGE/backend/app/routes/support_routes.py — Single POST /send endpoint; fires email to support@algonney.com; no DB storage, no ticket ID, no admin visibility
- /algonney/BRIDGE/backend/app/routes/custom_strategy_routes.py — Most complete ticket-like system in codebase; strategy_requests + custom_strategy_messages collections; full bidirectional message threading with unread counters, inbox integration, per-thread badge tracking
- /algonney/BRIDGE/backend/app/core/notification_websocket.py — NotificationWebSocketManager: Redis-backed, multi-device, heartbeat, missed-notification replay; send_inbox_update(), send_toast(), is_online()
- /algonney/BRIDGE/backend/app/services/inbox_state_service.py — InboxStateService: manages user_inbox_state + admin_inbox_state MongoDB collections; increment_user_unread(), mark_thread_read(), broadcast_inbox_update()
- /algonney/BRIDGE/backend/app/models/notification_models.py — UserInboxState (threads Dict, unread_by_category), AdminInboxState (threads Dict, unread_strategy_messages_total)
- /algonney/BRIDGE/backend/app/routes/notification_user_routes.py — GET /api/notifications/ws/{user_id} WebSocket; inbox fetch; mark-read
- /algonney/BRIDGE/backend/app/routes/admin_websocket_routes.py — AdminWSManager; WebSocket /admin/ws

Frontend:
- /algonney/BRIDGE/frontend/front/src/components/support/SupportWidget.tsx — Full chat-style UI shell; all state driven by useSupportWidget
- /algonney/BRIDGE/frontend/front/src/hooks/useSupportWidget.ts — All logic is local-only: localStorage persistence, simulated bot delay, startLiveChat() hardcoded with setTimeout fake agent response
- /algonney/BRIDGE/frontend/front/src/components/support/mockData.ts — All categories/topics are static mock data
- /algonney/BRIDGE/frontend/front/src/pages/SupportEmbedPage.tsx — Wraps /support URL in a full-page <iframe>
- /algonney/BRIDGE/frontend/front/src/components/ui/FloatingSupportButton.tsx — Launcher button for the widget
- /algonney/BRIDGE/frontend/front/src/services/notificationWebSocket.ts — NotificationWebSocketService: reconnection (12 attempts, exp backoff), heartbeat, subscribe()/unsubscribe()
- /algonney/BRIDGE/frontend/front/src/components/adminv2/AdminSidebar.tsx — Full sidebar nav definition; no Support or Tickets entry anywhere
- /algonney/BRIDGE/frontend/front/src/components/adminv2/sections/StrategyRequestsSection.tsx — Lists strategy requests from /admin/strategies; no message/reply UI
- /algonney/BRIDGE/frontend/front/src/components/adminv2/sections/NotificationsSection.tsx — Bulk notification management only; unrelated to support

---
FLOW:

Current (broken) support flow:
1. User opens SupportWidget → clicks "Live Chat" → startLiveChat() sets liveChatState: 'connecting'
2. After 1s setTimeout, hardcoded 'Hi! How can I help you today?' appears — no backend call made
3. User sends message → sendMessage() adds to local state, shows typing indicator → bot response removed from code (comment: "Bot responses would come from API - removed mock data")
4. Conversation persists to localStorage only — never reaches any server
5. Alternatively: user uses contact form → POST /api/support/send → email fires to support@algonney.com — no ticket created, no admin UI shows it

What exists for custom strategy (reusable pattern):
1. User submits POST /api/custom-strategy/submit → stored in strategy_requests MongoDB collection
2. Admin sees it in StrategyRequestsSection (thin list view)
3. Either party calls POST /api/custom-strategy/requests/{id}/messages → stored in custom_strategy_messages
4. On message send: InboxStateService.increment_user_unread() or increment_admin_unread() called → broadcast_inbox_update() pushes inbox state via WebSocket → unread badge updates in real-time
5. GET /api/custom-strategy/unread-summary available for polling fallback

---
EXISTING COMPONENTS:

┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Component │ Status │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ User WebSocket connection (frontend client + backend endpoint) │ ✅ Complete and production-ready │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Inbox unread counter infrastructure (user + admin) │ ✅ Complete — UserInboxState, AdminInboxState, thread-level tracking │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Real-time WebSocket push on message events │ ✅ Complete — broadcast_inbox_update() │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Thread-level unread tracking pattern │ ✅ Complete — in custom_strategy_routes.py │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Support widget UI shell (categories, chat, composer, bubbles) │ ✅ UI built, but 100% mocked │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ SupportEmbedPage + FloatingSupportButton │ ✅ Present │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Contact form email endpoint │ ✅ Works — fires email only │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ AdminV2 StrategyRequestsSection │ ✅ Thin list view (no message UI) │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Notification badge delivery (Toast, inbox_state push) │ ✅ Complete │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

---
MISSING PIECES:

1. No support ticket MongoDB model/collection — no SupportTicket, SupportMessage, or equivalent schema exists anywhere in backend/app/models/
2. No support ticket backend routes — no POST /api/support/tickets, no GET /api/support/tickets/{id}/messages, no admin equivalent
3. No AdminV2 Support section — zero nav entry, zero section component, zero hook for support tickets
4. SupportWidget is fully disconnected — sendMessage() never calls any API; startLiveChat() is pure simulation; all data is ephemeral localStorage
5. No reply-to-user mechanism — admin has no way to respond to any support contact (email-only, and the email has no tracking)
6. No ticket status lifecycle — no open/in_progress/resolved/closed state anywhere for support
7. No admin unread badge for support — AdminInboxState.threads exists but no support-specific counter feeds it
8. StrategyRequestsSection has no message thread UI — admin can see requests, but cannot send/read messages within AdminV2
9. SupportChatThread.tsx, SupportComposer.tsx, SupportMessageBubble.tsx — built components with no real data source

---
RISKS / NOTES:

- The custom_strategy_routes.py system is the closest reusable pattern — it has the full schema, threading, unread counter integration, and WebSocket push already wired. A support ticket system could reuse this pattern with a new collection and adapted routes rather than
building from scratch.
- NotificationWebSocketManager already handles multi-device, missed messages, and presence — it is fully reusable for support ticket push without modification.
- UserInboxState.threads already supports arbitrary thread_id → unread_count — a support ticket ID can slot directly into this without schema changes.
- The SupportWidget UI components (SupportChatThread, SupportComposer, SupportMessageBubble) are well-built and only need to be wired to real API calls.
- No database migrations are needed (MongoDB is schemaless); only new collections and routes.

---
RECOMMENDED_NEXT_CHECK:
Design the minimum new backend contract: define SupportTicket + SupportMessage document shapes, determine which existing route patterns to clone from custom_strategy_routes.py, and specify the exact AdminV2 sidebar entry + section + hook needed, before writing any code.

<<>>

✻ Baked for 3m 34s
history.claude_summary: The codebase has extensive real-time infrastructure (WebSocket, inbox state, unread counters) and a near-complete ticket-style system built for custom strategy requests, but zero dedicated support ticket system: the user-facing support widget is fully mocked/disconnected from any backend, the backend support route only emails a mailbox, and AdminV2 has no support/inbox section at all.
history.planner_interpretation: Before designing or changing architecture, we need a proven map of what already exists across frontend, backend, database, and AdminV2 for support/chat/help/contact flows. This establishes the current data path, prevents duplicate work, and reveals whether there is any existing ticketing, messaging, websocket/SSE/polling, or admin inbox logic to reuse safely.

history.claude_summary: The custom strategy system is a complete, production-quality support-ticket-like pattern with full bidirectional messaging, per-thread unread counters, WebSocket push, and admin inbox state — it can be cloned almost entirely for the new support ticket system with only domain-specific field renaming. No architectural invention is needed.
history.planner_interpretation: The highest-priority next step is to inspect the existing custom strategy request/message implementation in detail and extract the exact document shapes, route contract, unread-counter integration, and websocket update flow that can be safely reused for support tickets. This proves the minimal enterprise-grade backend architecture before any code changes.

history.claude_output:
SUMMARY:
All evidence is confirmed. The custom strategy system provides a complete copy-and-adapt blueprint; the only structural additions needed are new inbox counter fields on UserInboxState/AdminInboxState, new collection names, a clean status enum, and a new route file registered in
api_main.py. Everything else is a direct rename.

---
FILES:
- path: /algonney/BRIDGE/backend/app/models/notification_models.py — UserInboxState (line 211), AdminInboxState (line 245): exact current field names confirmed
- path: /algonney/BRIDGE/backend/app/models/payment_models.py — CustomStrategyRequest (line 369), CustomStrategyMessage (line 398), CustomStrategyRequestStatus (line 357): exact reuse source
- path: /algonney/BRIDGE/backend/app/routes/custom_strategy_routes.py — full messaging endpoint pattern
- path: /algonney/BRIDGE/backend/app/services/inbox_state_service.py — counter mutation logic; category="strategy_messages" hardcoded branch (lines 97–98, 185–186)
- path: /algonney/BRIDGE/backend/app/api_main.py — router registration: custom_strategy_router (line 534, no prefix), support_router (line 675, prefix /api/support)

---
SUPPORT_TICKET_SCHEMA:

Collection: support_tickets

{
"_id": "ObjectId",
"id": "string (set by route: str(_id))",

"user_id": "string",
"user_email": "string",
"user_display_name": "string|null",

"subject": "string (max 200)",
"category": "billing|technical|account|trading|other",
"priority": "low|normal|high|urgent",
"status": "open|pending_admin|pending_user|resolved|closed",
"source": "widget|contact_form|email|admin_created",

"assigned_admin_id": "string|null",
"assigned_admin_email": "string|null",
"assigned_at": "datetime|null",

"last_message_at": "datetime|null",
"last_message_preview": "string (first 100 chars)|null",
"last_message_by": "user|admin|null",

"resolved_at": "datetime|null",
"resolved_by_admin_id": "string|null",
"resolution_note": "string|null",

"closed_at": "datetime|null",
"close_reason": "resolved|abandoned|spam|duplicate|null",

"history": [
{
"status": "string",
"timestamp": "ISO datetime",
"previous_status": "string|null",
"actor_id": "string",
"actor_type": "user|admin",
"notes": "string|null"
}
],

"created_at": "datetime",
"updated_at": "datetime"
}

Index strategy: {user_id, status}, {status, last_message_at}, {assigned_admin_id, status}

---
SUPPORT_MESSAGE_SCHEMA:

Collection: support_messages

{
"_id": "ObjectId",
"ticket_id": "string (str(ObjectId) of support_tickets._id)",

"sender_id": "string (user_id or admin user_id)",
"sender_type": "user|admin",
"sender_name": "string (display name or 'Support Team')",

"body": "string (max 5000)",

"is_internal": false,

"read_by": ["user_id", ...],
"created_at": "datetime"
}

Notes:
- body preferred over message for clarity and to distinguish from Mongo reserved usage
- is_internal = false in v1 (admin-only notes deferred); field present but always false so v2 can add without migration
- No attachment support in v1 (deferred)
- read_by array identical to strategy messages pattern — already proven

Index strategy: {ticket_id, created_at} ascending

---
STATUS_ENUM:

class SupportTicketStatus(str, Enum):
open = "open" # New, not yet replied by admin
pending_admin = "pending_admin" # User has replied, waiting for admin
pending_user = "pending_user" # Admin has replied, waiting for user
resolved = "resolved" # Admin marked resolved
closed = "closed" # Permanently closed (no reopen)

---
STATUS_TRANSITION_RULES:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────┐
│ Event │ From │ To │ Actor │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
│ User creates ticket │ — │ open │ user │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
│ Admin replies to any open/pending ticket │ open OR pending_admin │ pending_user │ admin │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
│ User replies to any pending/resolved ticket │ pending_user OR resolved │ pending_admin │ user │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
│ Admin resolves ticket │ open OR pending_admin OR pending_user │ resolved │ admin │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
│ Admin closes ticket │ any except closed │ closed │ admin │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
│ User replies on closed │ closed │ BLOCKED — 400 error; user must open new ticket │ — │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
│ Admin re-opens ticket │ resolved │ open │ admin │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┤
│ Ticket auto-close │ resolved after 7 days with no reply │ closed │ system (deferred v2) │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────┘

Key rule: resolved tickets reopen to pending_admin when user replies. closed tickets do not reopen.

---
INBOX_UNREAD_CHANGES:

Category string to use: "support_messages" (passed as category= param to all inbox_state_service calls)

Changes required in UserInboxState (notification_models.py):
# ADD this field alongside unread_strategy_messages_total:
unread_support_messages_total: int = Field(default=0, description="Unread support ticket messages")

Changes required in AdminInboxState (notification_models.py):
# ADD this field alongside unread_strategy_messages_total:
unread_support_messages_total: int = Field(default=0, description="Unread user messages in support tickets")

Changes required in InboxStateService (inbox_state_service.py):

In increment_user_unread() — add branch after the strategy_messages branch:
elif category == "support_messages":
update_ops["$inc"]["unread_support_messages_total"] = amount

In increment_admin_unread() — add branch:
elif category == "support_messages":
update_ops["$inc"]["unread_support_messages_total"] = amount

Thread ID strategy: Use str(ticket._id) as thread_id — identical pattern to strategy requests.

Admin real-time gap: Currently admin unread counters have no WebSocket push — admin must poll GET /api/admin/support/unread-summary. This is acceptable for v1 (strategy system works the same way). Admin WS push is deferred to v2.

---
API_CONTRACT:

User endpoints (auth: get_current_user_id):

┌────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Method │ Path │ Request Body │ Response │
├────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ POST │ /api/support/tickets │ {subject, category, priority?, body, source?} │ {success, ticket_id, status: "open"} │
├────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ GET │ /api/support/tickets │ ?status&limit&offset │ {tickets:[{id,subject,category,priority,status,last_message_at,last_message_preview,unread_count,created_at}], total} │
├────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ GET │ /api/support/tickets/{ticket_id} │ — │ {ticket: {...full doc...}} │
├────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ POST │ /api/support/tickets/{ticket_id}/messages │ {body} │ {success, message: {...}} │
├────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ GET │ /api/support/tickets/{ticket_id}/messages │ — │ {messages:[...], total} — auto-marks read │
├────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ GET │ /api/support/tickets/{ticket_id}/unread-count │ — │ {unread_count: int} │

The custom strategy system provides a complete copy-and-adapt blueprint; the only structural additions needed are new inbox counter fields on UserInboxState/AdminInboxState, new collection names, a clean status enum, and a new route file registered in api_main.py. Everything else is a direct rename.
history.planner_interpretation: The reusable pattern is now proven.
